description:
The AM has marked this as a HIGH PRIORITY task that cannot be completed before this task: Edit Headshot to Match Meet the Legal Team Background Style - J229 Minton Law SEO

Domain: justinmintonlaw.com

Summary: Minton Law has two new attorneys to add to the "Meet the Legal Team" section on the homepage: Michael Graugnard and Oliver Cushing. 
Client Goal: Add two new attorneys to the homepage team section with photos that visually match the existing attorney photo style.

Requirements for Completion:
  • Update Oliver's crushing profile with a new image from this child ticket task.
  • The AM just reviewed Oliver's profile, and it doesn't include a link to his bio. If you don't have his bio details, they are copied below in this task. https://justinmintonlaw.com/legal-staff/#oliver-cushing
  • Michael Graugnard has been added to the Meet the Legal Team section on the homepage
  • Oliver Cushing has been added to the Meet the Legal Team section on the homepage
  • New attorney cards are consistent in formatting and styling with existing attorney cards
Deadline: No deadline stated.

Assets and References:
  • Photos for Michael Graugnard - https://justinmintonlaw.com/legal-staff/#michael-graugnard
  • Oliver Cushing profile - https://justinmintonlaw.com/legal-staff/#oliver-cushing
Oliver's Bio

Oliver Cushing is an attorney at Minton Law Firm, where his practice focuses on personal injury matters. He is responsible for evaluating new cases, working closely with clients during the critical early stages of their claims, and navigating the complexities of the pre-litigation process to ensure every case is positioned for success. He also provides essential legal support and training for the firm’s intake team.

Before joining Minton Law Firm, Mr. Cushing practiced in estate planning and elder law, where he advised clients on complex and high-net-worth estate plans, asset protection, and Medicaid planning strategies. That background sharpened his ability to analyze difficult situations, negotiate with large institutions, and carefully review important documents—skills he now uses to help personal injury clients protect their interests.

His earlier experience in hospitality management also strengthened the communication and client service approach he brings to his practice today:
“When people call our firm, they are often dealing with stress, confusion, and a lot of unanswered questions. My goal is to help make the process easier to understand and give people confidence that someone is listening and looking out for their best interests. I want clients to know they do not have to figure everything out on their own.”

Mr. Cushing was born in Memphis, Tennessee. He graduated as salutatorian from Gateway Christian Schools, received his bachelor's degree in legal studies magna cum laude from the University of Memphis, and earned his Juris Doctor from the University of Arkansas at Little Rock William H. Bowen School of Law. During his undergraduate studies, he served as Vice President of the Legal Students Association at the University of Memphis. While in law school, he was appointed to the Dean’s Student Advisory Board and also served in student leadership as Treasurer of the Student Bar Association, the Business & Tax Law Society, and the Sports & Entertainment Law Society.

Mr. Cushing has been practicing law since 2024 and is licensed to practice in Arkansas. He is also a member of the Arkansas Trial Lawyers Association. In addition to practicing law, he volunteers his time as a youth baseball coach in Maumelle. Outside of work, he enjoys exercise, being outdoors, collecting baseball cards, and studying history. He and his wife have two cats, Romeo and Louis.

title: Reporting Call Prep
description:
SECTION 2: PERFORMANCE OVERVIEW (CM SECTION)


  Please provide by Wednesday 12 PM:
  1. How is performance this month?
  2. Campaign health status: Green / Yellow / Red
  3. Areas of concern
  4. Anything stand out? Wins or things we need to be aware of?
  5. Do you need anything from the client?
  6. What is our main focus next month?
AM must review and confirm alignment with CM before call.

Risk Flag:
 ☐ No performance concerns
 ☐ Minor fluctuation
 ☐ Underperformance risk

If risk exists:
  • Has CM been aligned?
  • Has AM Lead been notified?
No surprises allowed on call.
 

This task must be fully updated by EOD Monday before the reporting call.

This document serves as:
  • Internal alignment between AM, CM, and SEO
  • Source of truth for call structure
  • Risk detection tool
  • Talking point preparation
No one should walk into the reporting call unprepared.

This prep aligns directly with the live call agenda.

AM should use this document to guide the call in real time.

No one should enter the call without this fully filled out.

SECTION 1: OPENING FRAME (AM)


What tone are we setting?

Client temperature this month:
 ☐ Strong
 ☐ Neutral
 ☐ Slightly concerned
 ☐ Frustrated

Notes from weekly pulse:


Any relationship items to acknowledge at opening?

Opening focus reminder:
What do we want the client to feel by minute 3?

SECTION 2: PERFORMANCE OVERVIEW (CM SECTION)


@LeadSEO please provide by Wednesday 12 PM:
  1. How is performance this month?
  2. Campaign health status: Green / Yellow / Red
  3. Areas of concern
  4. Anything stand out? Wins or things we need to be aware of?
  5. Do you need anything from the client?
  6. What is our main focus next month?
AM must review and confirm alignment with CM before call.

Risk Flag:
 ☐ No performance concerns
 ☐ Minor fluctuation
 ☐ Underperformance risk

If risk exists:
  • Has CM been aligned?
  • Has AM Lead been notified?
No surprises allowed on call.

SECTION 3: PERFORMANCE Q&A PREP


List anticipated performance questions:




Confirm:

☐ CM prepared for these
☐ Messaging aligned
☐ Time boundary plan set

SECTION 4: WORK COMPLETED (AM SECTION)


This mirrors the “Campaign Execution” portion of the call.

List in client-friendly language:
  • SEO deliverables completed:

  • Content published:

  • Links built:

  • Technical fixes:

  • Dev completed:

  • Wins (Sentinel, visibility gains, authority growth):

No internal jargon.

SECTION 5: WORK IN PROGRESS (AM)


  • Active SEO initiatives:

  • Content pipeline:

  • Technical initiatives:

  • Dev in progress:

  • Strategic pushes:

Must clearly show momentum.

SECTION 6: TEAM NEEDS (AM)


Do we need anything from the client?
  • Approvals:

  • Content:

  • Access:

  • Decisions:


If nothing needed, state clearly: None at this time.

SECTION 7: MONTH AHEAD VISION (AM)


This section drives confidence.

Answer clearly:
  • What is our main focus next month?
  • What are we monitoring closely?
  • What would success look like by next reporting call?

SECTION 8: FINAL Q&A PREP (AM)


Non-performance questions the client may raise:


Plan response or follow-up pathway.

Confirm:

☐ If unsure, defer and follow up
☐ CM will not re-enter unless pre-discussed

SECTION 9: ESCALATION CHECK


Before call, confirm:

☐ SEO updates received
☐ Dev updates confirmed
☐ Any blockers escalated
☐ AM Lead notified if risk exists
☐ CM aligned on any sensitive topics

If any box cannot be checked, escalate before call.

SECTION 10: CALL CONTROL REMINDERS (AM)


  • Set agenda at beginning
  • Protect time at minute 20
  • Transition clearly
  • Do not guess
  • Close confidently

Hi, this is inline w/ a group of tasks we have from a sheet. Here is the link for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.

title: Query about Website addition
description:
Hi, seeking help on how we can ask the Dev team with a change request we got for Justin Minton. Please see info below:

Justin is running a new campaign where he encourages callers to "Dial 7," and he's like his website to reflect that by updating his phone number in the top right corner. I mocked this up and Megan says it looks good; they're not very picky, so we have some wiggle room here.

In this mockup, the DIAL 7 is 56 px and the hex code for the 7 is #db0000. The bottom copy "FOR FREE CONSULTATION" is unchanged.


Thanks much!

Proving medical malpractice can be complex, but it’s essential if you’ve suffered due to a healthcare provider’s negligence. At Minton Law Firm, we’re dedicated to helping victims understand and build strong cases. Medical malpractice occurs when a medical professional fails to meet an accepted standard of care, resulting in harm. Proving these elements often requires substantial evidence, including medical records, physician statements, and expert testimony. While some cases may resolve through settlement, others may need to go to trial for full compensation.

description:
You may have noticed that compensation packages in personal injury cases can differ significantly. Some individuals receive six-figure settlements or more, while others get considerably less. That's because each case is distinct, with factors like liability, injury severity, and potential earnings influencing the total compensation.

Given these variables, determining fair compensation is both a science and an art. If you depend on an insurance company to calculate your compensation, you might not receive enough to rebuild your life. Having a dedicated personal injury attorney calculate and fight for the compensation you deserve increases your chances of success.

At Minton Law Firm, we will examine crucial factors that could impact your compensation and discuss the tools used by insurers and lawyers to calculate these amounts, enabling you to fight for what you deserve. Learn more, here: https://justinmintonlaw.com/calculating-your-potential-compensation/

description:
Losing a loved one due to someone else's negligence or intentional act can be devastating, compounded by financial hardships. Families can file a wrongful death claim for compensation, which can ease financial burdens and help them move forward. Consulting our empathetic and experienced wrongful death lawyers promptly is crucial. We will help you navigate the legal process, gather necessary evidence, and ensure claims are filed within the allowed timeframe. Essential evidence includes death certificates, medical records, police reports, and witness testimonies. Proving negligence and liability is critical. Damages sought may include medical and funeral expenses, lost earnings, and emotional suffering. Settlements are often negotiated, but if unsuccessful, a lawsuit may be filed. At Minton Law Firm, we offer compassionate legal support to help families secure financial stability after such tragic losses. title: Add GBP Post
description:
Slip and fall injuries, accounting for over a million emergency room visits annually in the U.S., can drastically change lives, particularly when they occur in places of business. In Arkansas, victims may consider filing a claim if they can establish that their injuries resulted from a hazardous condition on the property, invoking premises liability laws. Proving such claims requires demonstrating that the property owner was aware of the dangerous condition and failed to rectify it, leading to the injury. Essential to building a strong case is the collection of comprehensive evidence, including photos, eyewitness accounts, and medical records. Arkansas's modified comparative negligence law also plays a crucial role, as victims must prove they were less than 50% responsible for the accident to claim damages. Given the complexities of proving fault and negotiating with insurance adjusters, hiring an experienced slip and fall attorney can be invaluable. Our premises liability attorneys will assist in gathering evidence, negotiating settlements, and, if necessary, taking the case to trial, allowing victims to focus on recovery. Learn more: https://justinmintonlaw.com/can-you-sue-a-business-for-a-slip-and-fall-in-arkansas/

#PersonalInjuryAttorney #JustinMintonLaw #PremisesLiability  #freeconsultation

Our personal injury attorneys prioritize these factors in your wrongful death claim or lawsuit, guiding you through essential steps to maximize your settlement. title: Add GBP Post
description:
The idea of comparative negligence recognizes that accidents often result from the careless actions of multiple parties. For example, if you were in an accident where the other driver ran a red light while you were speeding, both of you might share some responsibility for the incident. Different states have various rules for shared fault. In vehicle accident cases, the US uses contributory negligence, pure comparative fault, and modified comparative negligence rules to determine liability. Arkansas follows the modified comparative negligence system. Under this law, you may be able to file an insurance claim and receive a reduced amount of compensation from the other driver, even if you are partially responsible for the traffic accident. Learn more: https://justinmintonlaw.com/understanding-shared-fault-laws/

title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg 
President 
Corey@clixsy.com 
801-645-6466

title: Discovery Meeting
description:
Follow the Discovery Meeting Agenda prepared before.

Remember to ask for referrals!

Ask if they know anyone else you should talk to who is facing similar issues or runs a similar type of business.  Record any referred leads in the form field below.

Transfer all Referrals to CRM for follow up.

Referrals Contact Sheet uploaded here.

title: Email Discovery Meeting Agenda to client
Prepare Discovery Meeting Agenda
description:
DISCOVERY MEETING INTERNAL CHECKLIST


Have you discussed the Client's Business/Campaign Goals? One goal per campaign.

Have you built a Typical Customer Journey for the Client's business?

Have you built a Typical Buyer Persona for each campaign?

Have you decided on Campaign KPIs to measure our success?

Have you agreed on Monthly KPI Targets?

Have you set Campaign Budgets?

Have you spoken about common objections to sales the Client has experienced?

Have you done a SWOT analysis on each of the Client's Top 3 competitors?

Has Client intimated any legal constraints to advertising?

Have you discussed Integrated Marketing and looked at Client's past and current advertising campaigns?

Has the Client brought images/logos etc.?

Have you brainstormed ideas for creatives/messages/slogans/copy etc.?

Have you suggested any further services that could be beneficial to the Client?

If the CPC has changed have you transferred changes to Final Client Contract (FCC)?

Have you agreed the new Price & Payment Terms?

Have you written up the Final Client Contract (FCC) while Client is still in the office?

Have you got the Final Client Contract (FCC) signed?

Have you discussed incentives/discounts for referrals?

Have you asked for referrals? Add the on the next sheet.
